overview: Dr. NTR College of Agricultural Engineering (CAE), Bapatla, established in 1983, is a constituent college of Acharya N.G. Ranga Agricultural University (ANGRAU). It is the oldest and one of the most prestigious agricultural engineering colleges in Andhra Pradesh, dedicated to the development of farm machinery, soil and water conservation, and post-harvest technology. The campus is known for its research-driven academic environment and its historical significance as a leader in technical agricultural education.
accreditation: Accredited by ICAR (Indian Council of Agricultural Research). Affiliated with Acharya N.G. Ranga Agricultural University (ANGRAU). Approved by AICTE.

coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45-50% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: M.Tech (Farm Machinery & Power Engineering, Soil & Water Engineering, etc.)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: B.Tech in Agricultural Engineering + GATE / AP PGECET score
Post-graduation in relevant field with 50% aggregate

placements.internationalOffers: Occasional (primarily via global NGOs or food tech firms)

admissions.admissionProcess: 70% of seats are under the Convener Quota (Category A) based on AP EAPCET ranks. The university offers a specific reservation for candidates from agricultural backgrounds (Farmer Quota). Counseling is conducted by ANGRAU. National quota seats (15-20%) are filled through ICAR AIEEA.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026: May 12–18, 2026; Registration Deadline: April 2026; Results: Late May 2026; Counseling: July 2026.

facilities: Separate Hostels for Boys and Girls with Wi-Fi
Central Library with Digital Resource Center
campus.specialNote: The college is a recognized center for 'Farm Machinery Testing' for the Government of India. It also hosts the RAWE (Rural Agricultural Work Experience) program, which provides students with intensive on-field residency and interaction with the farming community.
